Add star shop with permanent perks, synergies, and gameplay hooks

Star shop (new tab after first evolution):
- 5 categories: Speed, Luck, Buffs, Evolution, Synergies
- Sequential unlock within each category (must buy in order)
- Stars are spent (consumed) creating strategic trade-off vs passive +5% DPS

15 perks total:
- Cooldown reduction (-15%/-30%), auto-click (1/sec)
- Lucky bugs 50% more frequent, 2x bonus rewards
- Buff duration 60s, buff effects +50% stronger
- Evolution start bonus (1min/10min DPS)
- 4 animal synergy sets + Dierenrijk (all synergies bonus)

Synergies boost only the animals in the set, not global DPS.
All perks persist through evolution. Buff strength affects
DPS multiplier, sale discount, and click DPS bonus.

Also: daily challenges collapse to compact one-liner when all done.

commit.message:
fix: integrate GitHub answers into clarifications.md before gate check (#348)

The clarify phase gate (`on-questions`) checks the local clarifications.md
for pending questions. When the Claude CLI clarify command fails to persist
GitHub answers back into the file, the gate re-activates and the workflow
loops indefinitely at the clarification step.

Add a defensive `integrateClarificationAnswers()` step that runs before
the gate evaluation: it fetches issue comments, parses Q/A patterns, and
writes answers into clarifications.md so `hasPendingClarifications()`
sees the updated state.

Co-authored-by: christrudelpw <chris@generacy.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement XorLinkedList (Phase 1 - Lists & Queues)

Implements a memory-efficient doubly linked list using XOR of
previous and next pointers. Each node stores only one pointer
instead of two, saving 50% pointer memory overhead.

Features:
- O(1) push/pop operations at both ends
- O(n) traversal with iterator protocol
- Comprehensive test suite with stress tests
- Memory leak detection via std.testing.allocator
- Invariant validation

Tests: 8 passing
- Basic operations
- Push/pop front/back
- Mixed operations
- Iterator
- Validate
- Stress test (1000 elements)
- Memory leak detection
- Single element edge case

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: standardize all API responses with consistent success/error shape

Add api/response.py with api_success() and api_error() helpers.
All endpoints now return:
  success: { status_code, status: "success", message, data }
  error:   { status_code, status: "error", message, error }

Also add global HTTPException and unhandled exception handlers in main.py
so every error path returns the same shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Azure AD SSO authentication with JWT-protected endpoints

Implement end-to-end auth: backend OAuth2 flow exchanges Azure AD auth
codes for Gaia JWTs via Microsoft Graph, protects /api/query and
/api/audit with Bearer token middleware. Frontend wires up NextAuth.js
v5 with MicrosoftEntraID provider, session-aware API client, and
sign-in/sign-out UI in the header.

commit.message:
docs(nzbdav): address review findings and incorporate official setup guide

Major changes from studying the official NZBDav setup guide and
incorporating findings from 4 review agents:

Architecture:
- Switch from network_mode: host to bridge networking with
  127.0.0.1 port mapping — NZBDav has no PORT env var (Issue #227),
  making host networking incompatible with dynamic port allocation
- Add extra_hosts for host.docker.internal arr connectivity
- Add /mnt:/mnt:rslave volume for mount propagation (symlink
  resolution and library repairs require container to see host mount)
- Keep ALL rclone flags (official guide documents each as essential)

Security:
- umask 077 subshell for credential file writes (TOCTOU race fix)
- rclone obscure via stdin (password not in ps aux)
- unset webdav_pass after use
- chmod 700 on config directory (SQLite contains Usenet creds)
- No UFW rule needed (127.0.0.1 binding via bridge networking)

Simplification:
- Replace --setup-rclone subcommand with re-run detection
- Replace arr discovery with static connection info text
- Simplify rclone.conf removal to file deletion (app-isolated)
- Remove mountpoint -q check that would break re-runs
- Add bounded ExecStartPre timeout (60s max)
- Concrete sub_filter rules with Accept-Encoding header

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: skip invisible meshes in all raycasting and box-select paths

Hidden CSG source nodes (visible=false) were participating in hit-tests
because Three.js raycasting does not automatically skip invisible meshes.
This caused workplane placement to snap to the normal of a hidden input
shape rather than the visible boolean-result surface.

Filter to m.visible before intersectObjects in useWorkplanePlacement and
useRaycasting, and add an early-return guard in the useBoxSelect loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix panic on multi-byte UTF-8 chars in channel/EPG name truncation

String slicing like &s[..n] panics when n falls inside a multi-byte
character (e.g. ᵁᴴᴰ). Use a safe truncate_str helper that backs up
to the nearest char boundary.

commit.message:
Classify Pragma_Resetall_NoError → §22.3 [skip ci]

The test exercises `pragma resetall, but the test comment misidentifies the clause as §22.11.1. The LRM §22.3 is titled "`resetall" and defines the `resetall compiler directive — when encountered, all compiler directives are reset to their default values. `pragma is a separate directive covered in §22.11. The test uses `pragma resetall, which is the pragma form of resetall; however, the `resetall directive itself is defined in §22.3, and `pragma resetall is the pragma-equivalent spelled out in §22.11 (pragma). Checking §22.11 in the LRM confirms it covers the `pragma directive generally, with no subclause §22.11.1 for resetall specifically. The most precise and correct clause for the `resetall behavior (resetting all directives to defaults, no error) is §22.3.

Add user authentication (register, login, logout)

- Passwords hashed with Node crypto.scrypt
- Session store backed by PostgreSQL via connect-pg-simple

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix VAD create_response race condition causing agent silence after greeting

The create_response was set to false at session start to prevent auto-greeting,
but re-enablement had a race condition: if the greeting audio finished after the
3s grace period expired, create_response stayed false forever. Now uses a
vadReenabled flag to guarantee exactly one re-enable, with safety fallback in
the grace period timer.

speech. One voice at a time.

commit.message:
fix(win10): asarUnpack + import dinamico NER — v1.1.4

Fix 1: electron-builder.config.js — pattern asarUnpack per onnxruntime-node
esteso con glob senza '/*' finale e pattern esplicito 'dist/**' per garantire
che dist/binding.js finisca in app.asar.unpacked. Su Windows 10, Electron non
fa il fallover automatico da asar verso asar.unpacked per i file JS — se
binding.js è dentro asar, __dirname punta dentro asar e dlopen() fallisce.

Fix 2: nerService.ts — import '@huggingface/transformers' convertito da
statico top-level a import() dinamico con try/catch in tryLoadTransformers().
Se onnxruntime non è caricabile, l'app non crasha ma continua con sole regex
(CF, IBAN, P.IVA, email, telefono, pattern legali strutturati).
